title: Pulse Snake: Head or Tail
objective: Survive a 2-4 minute run by memorizing and replaying increasingly fast pulse sequences traveling across a snake's body, choosing safe or risky routes to keep your combo alive and post a leaderboard score.

controls.input: Tap the lit body segment when it is your next remembered beat
controls.outcome: The snake advances, the beat continues, and your combo builds

controls.input: Tap the flashing fork segment near the head or tail
controls.outcome: Head route gives a shorter safer sequence; tail route adds extra beats but boosts multiplier

controls.input: Hold one finger anywhere during the brief stun window
controls.outcome: You spend your stored combo shield to avoid an immediate fail once per run

steps: A short pulse pattern lights up along the snake from head to tail with distinct audio notes.
The pattern disappears and you tap the same segments back in order on the snake's body.
Every few sequences, a fork pulse appears and forces a choice: replay from the head for fewer beats or from the tail for a longer high-risk,
Successful replays make the snake longer and faster, adding more beats, tighter timing, and alternating directions.
A wrong tap, timeout, or broken order causes the snake to bite itself and ends the run unless you spend a saved combo shield.
scoring: Each correct tap scores points; finishing a full sequence raises a combo multiplier. Tail-route sequences add an instant multiplier bonus and a small leaderboard burst bonus. Missing a beat ends the run and submits your
tips: Watch the head first; sequence direction can reverse after a fork.
Use audio as much as visuals; each body zone has its own note.
Take head routes early to stabilize, then switch to tail routes once your combo is rolling.
Short holds are only for shield recovery; normal play is all quick taps.

trendContext.title: Playable Snake game in the browser – no downloads, works on mobile
